reviews.review_msg: Honestly don't know whether she realizes how unclear and unspecific with most if not all the assignments and tasks. Instead of providing clear expectations of the assignments and indicating deadlines on the syllabus, she makes them up as she goes. Highly disappointed in this course and in her teaching ability and wished I took it with another prof.

Lisa is as passionate as it comes, thought it is solely based on sustainability, the course has been applicable in real-life situations. This is by far the most "entertaining" course I have taken at SFU as it isn't just dry textbook reading, instead it is applying yourself and your skills. This being said, the group projects are a lot of work

Bus238 is the most difficult course I've ever taken. The material is a joke, I had three pages of class notes by the end of term - but 70% of your mark is in group projects or shadowing assignments. Your mark is barely based on your own merits. The professor is helpful, enthusiastic and friendly but the class itself is terrible. Do NOT take it.

Pretty unorganized, would say weird things and then blame the fact that its an 8:30 class. Projects were vague and then she took marks off for not including things that were not mentioned in the project description in the first place. Constantly talks about her 0 waste cafe in Downtown Vancouver. Hates when people walk in late. Class was meh overal
Good prof, enjoyed having her. Cares about the students on aspects outside of pure academics.
Lisa is a great prof, and enthusiastic about sustainability. Highly recommend!

I am wait for 1 semester to take this B2B courses. Lisa's focus is Sustainability and 0 waste strategy. Those topic are good, but we are taking B2B courses!!!   About the project, I learned nothing. But You have to write what you learned from B2B's readings and implement it in the B2C Project weekly.  I hope I haven't taken this class sincerely.

I took a lot from the course personally, but not academically. Lisa is wordy and unclear, her projects are on pre-determined unmovitational themes, and the exam was unethically handled where students were given all the time in the world until they felt they were ready. Take it for the credits, but grades aren't easy - so, don't take it.

I've been at SFU for 4 semesters so far and I can say she's the best prof I've ever had, hands down. Not the easiest marker but she's inspirational and really helpful. She's passionate about business and it's great talking to her about future opportunities and what not! She always offers help and I appreciate her detailed feedback on assignments :)
